title: Jet Ski Rental in Benalmadena: 2026 Prices, Tips & Best Spots
html:
Benalmadena offers some of the Costa del Sol's finest jet ski experiences, with departures from Puerto Deportivo. Expect to pay from €80 for a 30-minute guided tour to €250 for a full-hour rental. For a hassle-free booking, book a charter online through BoatHire24 and compare verified listings.

How much does it cost to rent a jet ski in Benalmadena?

Prices vary from €80 for a half-hour shared ride to €300 for a full-hour solo session. Most operators at Puerto Deportivo Benalmadena offer tiered packages. Shared tours are the cheapest, while private rentals include fuel and insurance. A one-hour guided trip typically costs €150, €250, and you can often negotiate for longer durations. For specific boat rentals like this speedboat, the price range is higher but includes a full skipper service.

Where to set off in Benalmadena

Most jet ski operators depart from Puerto Deportivo Benalmadena, a modern marina with full facilities. You can also launch from Puerto Marina Benalmadena, which is adjacent to the promenade. Other common departure points include the beaches of Santa Ana and Arroyo de la Miel. Popular routes head towards Torremolinos, Málaga's coastline, or the scenic Cabo de Gata. Many tours include a stop near the Isle of Tierra or the Cala del Moral.

Which jet ski to rent

  1. Standard jet ski (Yamaha VX or similar), best for beginners and short tours, seats 1-2, from €80 for 30 minutes
  2. Performance jet ski (Seadoo RXP), ideal for thrill-seekers on open water, seats 2-3, from €200 for one hour
  3. Two-person tourer (Sea-Doo GTI), comfortable for couples, seats 2, from €120 for one hour
  4. Family pack (two jet skis), allows a group to ride together, seats 4 total, from €300 per hour

What are the season and licensing rules?

Book early in July and August if you want a morning slot; the prevailing Levante wind can make afternoon seas choppy but also more exciting for experienced riders.

Benalmadena's high season runs from June to September, with peak demand in August. Most operators require a valid boat licence for jet skis over 15hp, but some offer short-term licences or include a guided tour where no licence is needed. The exact requirements depend on your nationality and the operator's insurance, so always confirm with the local company.

Costs of jet ski rental in Benalmadena

  • Shared guided tour (30 min): from €80 per person
  • Private half-day (2 hours): from €250 for one jet ski
  • Full-day rental (8 hours, with break): from €500 for a performance model
  • Private tour with snorkel stop (3 hours): typically €350, €450

At a glance

  • Price range: €80, €500
  • Peak months: June, September
  • Trip length: 30 minutes to 8 hours
  • Capacity: 1, 2 passengers per jet ski
  • Currency: EUR
  • Licence: Usually required, but guided tours may waive it

Questions about jet ski rental in Benalmadena

Do I need a licence to rent a jet ski in Benalmadena?

The requirement depends on the jet ski's power and the operator's policy, but most tours are licenced so you can ride without your own licence if accompanied.

Can I rent a jet ski without prior experience?

Yes, many operators at Puerto Deportivo offer guided sessions that include basic training and a lead guide at no extra cost.

What is the best time of day to rent a jet ski in Benalmadena?

Morning trips between 9am and noon offer calmer seas, while afternoon slots from 4pm to sunset provide stunning coastal views but windier conditions.

Are there any age restrictions for jet ski rental?

Drivers must be at least 18 years old and hold a valid ID; passengers must be at least 12, and life jackets are mandatory for all.

Benalmadena's jet ski rentals are perfect for exploring the Costa del Sol from a unique perspective. For more options, browse the listings on BoatHire24 to find the ideal watercraft. If you own a boat, you can list your own and reach thousands of travellers.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Benalmadena and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
